description_long: This object consists of four large, rectangular stone blocks arranged in a staggered, upright formation. Each block varies slightly in height and width, with sharp, clean edges and flat surfaces, suggesting they have been precisely cut. The blocks display a uniform, medium to dark gray coloration, typical of granite or concrete, and their surfaces appear matte with a slightly rough texture. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering, indicating they are either new or well-maintained. The blocks are closely grouped but not physically connected, creating narrow gaps between them. Their substantial size and mass suggest they are intended as robust barriers or obstacles in an urban environment.
description: A group of four upright, rectangular stone blocks with clean edges and a matte gray, slightly rough surface, arranged closely together as a barrier.
description_view_0: Four stone blocks are visible, with the two in the foreground appearing taller and wider, and the two in the background partially obscured.
description_view_1: The blocks are seen more side-on, revealing the narrowest block in the center and the relative spacing between all four blocks.
description_view_2: The largest block dominates the foreground, with the other three blocks receding into the background, showing their varying heights.
description_view_3: The arrangement of the blocks is clear, with the tallest and widest block on the left and the others forming a staggered line to the right.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a protective barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and unaged
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: closely grouped but visually isolated from other structures
